name: Transformer Models
tagline: Sequence modeling with attention mechanisms that scale to large datasets
problem:
Many tasks require modeling long-range dependencies in sequences: language, code, and time series. Earlier architectures struggle with long contexts, training instability, and limited parallelism.

Transformers solve this by using attention to connect tokens directly and by enabling highly parallel training on modern accelerators.
overview:
A transformer encodes tokens into vectors, applies self-attention to mix information across positions, then uses feed-forward layers and residual connections to build deep representations. Training typically uses gradient descent with backpropagation on large corpora.

Scaling behavior depends on data, compute, and optimization. The Codex treats the training recipe as part of the technology: tokenization, objective choice, evaluation, and safety constraints are not optional extras.

principles.name: Self-attention
principles.explanation: Computes weighted combinations of token representations based on content similarity.

principles.name: Residual learning
principles.explanation: Skip connections stabilize deep networks and preserve gradient flow.

principles.name: Parallel training
principles.explanation: Attention layers enable parallel computation across sequence positions, improving throughput.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Large-scale datasets
rawMaterials.purpose: Training data for language, code, or other sequences
earthLocations: Public web corpora, code repositories, curated datasets
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early off-world: limited corpora; rely on curated technical manuals and local logs; avoid unsafe or low-quality data sources.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Deduplication, filtering, licensing review, and safety red-teaming.

rawMaterials.name: Accelerated compute (GPUs/TPUs)
rawMaterials.purpose: Efficient training and inference for large models
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Power/thermal limits force smaller models; prioritize efficiency and distillation.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Cooling, power delivery, cluster networking, and monitoring.

buildSteps.title: Define objective and dataset
buildSteps.description: Choose language modeling or task-specific training; curate data with provenance and safety filters.

buildSteps.title: Choose architecture and scale
buildSteps.description: Pick model size to match compute/data; start smaller to validate pipeline and evaluation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Scaling without evaluation produces expensive confusion.

buildSteps.title: Train with checkpointing
buildSteps.description: Train with stable optimization settings; checkpoint frequently; log metrics and samples.
buildSteps.warningNote: Without checkpoints, failures waste weeks of compute.

buildSteps.title: Evaluate and red-team
buildSteps.description: Measure performance; probe for hallucinations, bias, and unsafe behaviors; iterate on data and constraints.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not rely on single benchmark scores.

buildSteps.title: Deploy with monitoring and limits
buildSteps.description: Add guardrails; monitor for drift; document known failure modes.
buildSteps.warningNote: Unbounded deployment can cause real-world harm.

history.event: Transformer architecture introduced for sequence modeling.
Systems engineering for training/inference becomes central.

impact:
Transformers enable strong performance across language and perception tasks and make large-scale model training a systems engineering discipline. They also raise new governance and safety problems due to capability concentration.

In The Codex tree, transformers sit near the top of the computing branch: they require stable compute, data, evaluation rigor, and safety processes to be useful rather than dangerous.
